title: YOUTH AND FAMILY EDUCATION ENGAGEMENT PREK-12 - 211info
text: EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO YOUTH AND FAMILY EDUCATION ENGAGEMENT PREK-12 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a neighborhood hub based in public schools that supports students and families. Services include academic help, skill-building, enrichment activities, family support, and access to health and social services. Also host community events and help students with school transitions and college or career planning. This program is a partnership between Multnomah County, the City of Portland, nonprofits, and local school districts. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Families in David Douglas and Gresham-Barlow. If child is not a student at one of the participating schools, staff will help determine which location to go to. Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 669-8350 Main phone (503) 232-4448 Hotline UNICA Crisis Line/Linea de Crisis UNICA Main phone (503) 669-8350 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Families can also walk in or call the school for information. FEES: Varies by service. Financial assistance available. DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: David Douglas and Gresham-Barlow School Districts Services: School Based Integrated Services * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - FOOD JUSTICE HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY RENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - EVICTION PREVENTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOME FINDER SERVICES INFORMATION SERVICES - INFORMATION AND REFERRAL LEGAL SERVICES - FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - UNICA UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
